evals.prompt: Build me a settings panel with a dark mode toggle and a modal for confirming account deletion.
evals.expected_output: Uses native <dialog> with showModal/close, proper focus move on open and return-to-trigger on close, Escape-to-close, aria-labelledby on the dialog. Dark mode uses prefers-color-scheme / light-dark() rather than only a manual class toggle. Toggle control is a real checkbox/button with visible state, not an unlabeled div.

evals.prompt: Create a product filter sidebar with a dropdown multi-select and a live-updating result count.
evals.expected_output: Multi-select uses either native <select multiple> or a correctly structured combobox/listbox ARIA pattern with keyboard support. Result count uses aria-live="polite" region that exists in the DOM before updates, not injected fresh each time. No aria-hidden misuse.

evals.prompt: Write a simple contact form with client-side validation.
evals.expected_output: Every input has an associated <label> (not placeholder-only). Uses native validation attributes (required, type="email", pattern) as first layer. Errors are associated via aria-describedby and announced via aria-live. Focus states use :focus-visible. Submit button is a real <button>. Validity styling uses :user-invalid/:user-valid rather than bare :invalid, so errors don't show before the user has interacted with the field.

evals.prompt: Build a toolbar overflow menu: a button that opens a menu of extra actions, plus a separate button in that menu that closes it.
evals.expected_output: Menu is a popover-attribute element with role="menu" (popovers have no inherent role). Open/close buttons use command/commandfor (e.g. command="toggle-popover"/"hide-popover") rather than JS click handlers calling showPopover()/hidePopover(). Menu items are role="menuitem" with arrow-key navigation and typeahead; Escape closes and returns focus to the trigger.

evals.prompt: Build a client-side-routed multi-page app shell with a nav sidebar that swaps the main content without a full page reload.
evals.expected_output: Uses the Navigation API (navigation.addEventListener('navigate', ...) + event.intercept()) rather than manually wiring click handlers, history.pushState(), and popstate. Focus is managed on navigation (e.g. moved to the new main content or its heading) rather than left on the now-stale trigger link.

evals.prompt: Build a tag/label picker where users choose a background color for a tag and the tag text needs to stay readable.
evals.expected_output: Text color on the tag is computed with the CSS contrast-color() function (color: contrast-color(var(--tag-color))) rather than a hardcoded color, a JS luminance calculation, or always-black/white text. Includes a plain fallback color declaration before it for browsers without support.
